title: Aurora Cannabis Announces Fiscal 2025 Second Quarter Results
text: Cannabis Business Times covers Aurora Cannabis fiscal 2025 second quarter results. Revenue performance: Q2 FY2025 total net revenue $88.5 million (30% YoY growth). Medical cannabis revenue strong with international sales particularly robust. Consumer cannabis declined as expected with strategic de-emphasis. Profitability: Adjusted EBITDA positive continuing company's streak of positive quarterly adjusted EBITDA. Multiple quarters of profitability demonstrate business model sustainability. Medical cannabis margins remained elevated at 69-70% range. Growth drivers: Global medical cannabis revenue growth 41% YoY in quarter. International medical revenue more than doubled year-over-year. Canada medical cannabis growth driven by insured patient segment. International markets: Germany experiencing strong demand post-descheduling. Australia building on MedReleaf platform. Poland and UK showing momentum. Management commentary: CEO emphasized disciplined approach to profitable growth. Focus on high-margin medical products over volume. International expansion executing well. Guidance: Positive free cash flow expected. Strong margins anticipated. Medical segment growth momentum expected to continue. Strategic direction: Medical-first positioning resonating with market. Profitability inflection becoming visible. Balance sheet strength enabling M&A opportunities. Analyst implications: Revenue growth trajectory supporting valuation case. Adjusted EBITDA sustainability demonstrating unit economics. Path to GAAP profitability becoming clearer.
